expectations.text: Plan contains a scaffolding milestone that creates README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, and AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: Milestone 0 includes steps to create README.md, PROJECT.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, TESTING.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CHANGELOG.md, AGENTS.md, and ADR-0001

expectations.text: Plan specifies a structured JSON envelope for all CLI command responses
expectations.evidence: Defines Envelope<T> TypeScript interface with ok, command, result, errors, warnings, metrics, schema_version, request_id

expectations.text: Plan defines an error code taxonomy with ERR_ prefixed codes
expectations.evidence: Lists 10 error codes: ERR_VALIDATION_REQUIRED, ERR_IO_CONNECTION, ERR_IO_FILE_NOT_FOUND, ERR_CONFLICT_FINGERPRINT, ERR_CONFLICT_ALREADY_APPLIED, ERR_CONFLICT_NOTHING_TO_ROLLBACK, ERR_IO_TRANSACTION, ERR_AUTH_FORBIDDEN, ERR_INTERNAL_BUG, ERR_VALIDATION_EMPTY_NAME

expectations.text: Plan specifies distinct exit code ranges per error category (not just 0/1)
expectations.evidence: Maps ranges: 0=success, 10=validation, 20=permission, 40=conflict, 50=I/O, 90=internal

expectations.text: Plan includes a guide subcommand that returns CLI schema as JSON
expectations.evidence: Milestone 1 step 5: Implement dbmig guide returning JSON with commands, error_codes, exit_codes, schema_version, compatibility

expectations.text: Plan adds --dry-run support to mutating commands (apply, rollback)
expectations.evidence: Both apply and rollback commands include --dry-run with structured change preview

expectations.text: Plan includes change records with before/after state for mutations
expectations.evidence: Apply and rollback commands return change records with type, target, before, after, impact fields

expectations.text: Plan includes fingerprint-based conflict detection for the plan/apply pattern
expectations.evidence: Apply command computes SHA-256 fingerprint of migration files + _migrations table state, rejects on mismatch with ERR_CONFLICT_FINGERPRINT

expectations.text: Plan mentions LLM=true environment variable support
expectations.evidence: Milestone 1 step 6 covers isatty() + LLM=true + --output flag with precedence: flags > env vars > isatty()

notes: With-skill achieves 100% pass rate across all 26 assertions (10+7+9) vs 61.5% without skill (16/26)
The webapp eval (scaffolding-only) shows no differentiation — both configurations score 7/7. This is expected since scaffolding is straightforward and does not require the CLI manifest.

The biggest differentiation is on Eval 1 (CLI tool) where with-skill scores 10/10 vs 4/10 without. The baseline misses AGENTS.md, ERR_ codes, guide command, change records, fingerprinting, and LLM=true.
Eval 3 (partial CLI) shows strong differentiation on mutation safety: with-skill adds dry-run, backup, and isatty() which the baseline misses entirely.
The with-skill runs use ~70% more tokens and take ~30% longer, which is the expected cost of reading the reference documents and producing more comprehensive output.
